summary.brief: Gibson v. Soin. Medical malpractice case involving cardiology.

evidence_index.text: Diane Gibson had a history of back pain. In September 2015, she sought treatment from Dr. Soin, a pain management specialist who was employed by Ohio Pain Clinic.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Soin implanted a temporary spinal cord stimulator to alleviate Mrs. Gibson’s back pain. That device was replaced by a Stage II Spinal Cord Stimulator (SCS) on December 31, 2015.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: In January and February 2016, Mrs. Gibson underwent additional surgical procedures due to an infection at the surgical site and exposed wires from the stimulator.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On April 21, 2016, Dr. Soin re-implanted the SCS in Mrs. Gibson’s back. Prior to conducting the procedure, he ordered a blood test and an electrocardiogram (EKG).
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The bloodwork indicated that Mrs. Gibson had low potassium, and her EKG showed abnormal T waves and prolonged Q waves.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Two days later, on April 23, 2016, Mrs. Gibson died at home at the age of 64.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The coroner concluded that the cause of death was arteriosclerotic cardiovascular disease with bronchopneumonia contributing.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Utlak testified that he was familiar with the standard of care in the pain management surgical field with respect to presurgical cardiac screenings.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Utlak

evidence_index.text: The trial court did not abuse its discretion in concluding, pursuant to Civ.R. 601, that Dr. Utlak was not competent to give expert testimony as to the Defendants’ standard of care in this particular case.
evidence_index.speaker: Court conclusion

evidence_index.text: The trial court’s judgments will be affirmed.
evidence_index.speaker: Court conclusion

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Diane Gibson had a history of back pain and sought treatment from Dr. Soin, a pain management specialist, in September 2015.

focused_exam.system: Examination mentioned but findings not specified

requestables.name: Blood Test
requestables.description: Pre-surgical blood test to assess patient's readiness for surgery

reveal.clinical_significance: Increased risk of complications during surgery
reveal.decision_impact: Should prompt correction of electrolyte imbalance before proceeding with surgery

requestables.name: EKG
requestables.description: Pre-surgical electrocardiogram to assess cardiac function

reveal.result_summary: Abnormal T waves and prolonged Q waves
reveal.decision_impact: Should prompt cardiology consultation before proceeding with surgery

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents with chronic back pain

timeline_phases.name: Pre-Surgical Workup
timeline_phases.description: Pre-surgical testing conducted before re-implantation of spinal cord stimulator

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient scheduled for re-implantation of spinal cord stimulator
decision_points.prompt: Should the surgery proceed given the abnormal pre-surgical test results?

options.description: Proceed with surgery without further intervention
options.clinical_reasoning: Defendant proceeded despite abnormal test results

options.description: Consult cardiologist and correct electrolyte imbalance before surgery
options.clinical_reasoning: Standard of care requires addressing potential cardiac risks before surgery

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant proceeded with surgery without addressing abnormal test results

expected_action_court.description: Consult cardiologist and correct electrolyte imbalance before surgery
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony indicated this was the standard of care

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Proceeding with surgery without addressing abnormal test results increased risk of complications
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Consultation with a cardiologist and correction of electrolyte imbalance
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

patient_outcome.description: Patient died at home two days after surgery

key_findings: Directed verdict in favor of defendants
Court found expert testimony insufficient to establish breach of standard of care

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to consult cardiologist and correct electrolyte imbalance
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Consultation and correction could have mitigated risk

factual_timeline.event: Patient sought treatment from Dr. Soin for back pain
Pre-surgical blood test and EKG performed

tests_performed.result: Abnormal T waves and prolonged Q waves

allegations.allegation: Failure to review and properly evaluate presurgical testing

court_findings.finding: Directed verdict in favor of defendants
court_findings.legal_significance: Expert testimony insufficient to establish breach of standard of care
